name: 1NT Responses
description: Full 1NT response system with Stayman, Jacoby Transfers, Smolen, and natural bids

teaching.purpose: Find the best contract after partner opens 1NT: major-suit fit via Stayman, transfers, or Smolen, or notrump game/invite
teaching.whenToUse: Partner opens 1NT (15-17 HCP balanced). You choose between Stayman (4-card major, 8+ HCP), Jacoby Transfer (5+ card major), Smolen (5-4 in majors, game values — bid Stayman then show pattern), or natural NT bids (no major).
whenNotToUse: 0-7 HCP with no 5-card major — pass
5+ card major with no 4-card in the other major — use transfer, not Stayman
5-4 in majors with invite values (8-9 HCP) — use Stayman, then show the 5-card major after 2D denial
4333 shape with 8-9 HCP — 2NT invite may be better than Stayman
teaching.tradeoff: Artificial bids (2♣ Stayman, 2♦/2♥ transfers, Smolen 3H/3S) give up natural meanings of those bids
teaching.principle: Finding an 8-card major fit is worth more than notrump; Stayman, transfers, and Smolen are tools to find that fit while keeping the strong hand as declarer
teaching.roles: Responder is captain after 1NT — opener describes, responder decides the final contract

name: Stayman
description: Find a 4-4 major fit after 1NT opening

teaching.purpose: Find a 4-4 major-suit fit after partner opens 1NT using the 2♣ Stayman bid
teaching.whenToUse: Partner opens 1NT (15-17 HCP balanced). You have 8+ HCP and a 4-card major (no 5-card major).
whenNotToUse: 5+ card major — use Jacoby Transfer instead
Under 8 HCP — pass
No 4-card major — bid 2NT (invite) or 3NT (game) directly
teaching.principle: 2C asks opener to show a 4-card major; responder then places the contract based on fit
teaching.roles: Responder is captain — asks opener to describe, then decides the final contract

name: Jacoby Transfers
description: Ensure the strong hand declares in a major-suit contract

teaching.purpose: Transfer the contract to opener's hand when responder has a 5+ card major after 1NT opening
teaching.whenToUse: Partner opens 1NT (15-17 HCP balanced). You have a 5+ card major suit.
whenNotToUse: Only a 4-card major — use Stayman instead
No major suits — bid notrump directly
teaching.principle: 2D transfers to hearts, 2H transfers to spades — opener always accepts, making the strong hand declarer
teaching.roles: Responder initiates the transfer; opener mechanically accepts; responder then decides the final contract level

name: Bergen Raises (Bundle)
description: Constructive, limit, game, preemptive, and splinter raises after a 1M opening

teaching.purpose: Show the right level of support when partner opens 1 of a major and you have 4+ card fit
teaching.whenToUse: Partner opens 1H or 1S and you have 4+ cards in their major. Choose the raise level based on HCP: preemptive (0-6), constructive (7-10), limit (10-12), game (13+), or splinter (12+ with shortage).
whenNotToUse: Fewer than 4 cards in partner's major — look for other bids
Passed hand — Bergen raises are off after a passed hand
After opponent interference — standard raises apply instead
teaching.tradeoff: 3C and 3D lose their natural minor-suit meanings in favor of showing major support
teaching.principle: With a known trump fit, bid to the level your combined strength supports — preempt with weakness, invite with middle values, bid game with strength
teaching.roles: Opener evaluates whether the partnership has game after responder's Bergen bid

name: DONT (Bundle)
description: Competitive overcalls showing distributional hands against opponent's 1NT

teaching.purpose: Compete against opponent's 1NT opening with distributional hands, showing two-suited or single-suited holdings via conventional bids and doubles
teaching.whenToUse: After opponent opens 1NT in direct seat: bid 2H with both majors (5-4+), 2D with diamonds + a major, 2C with clubs + a higher suit, 2S with 6+ natural spades, or double with any single long suit (6+, not spades).
whenNotToUse: In balancing seat (only direct seat overcall implemented)
With a balanced hand lacking distributional shape
With 4-4 or 5-3 shape (minimum 5-4 for two-suited, 6+ for single-suited)
teaching.tradeoff: DONT sacrifices natural penalty doubles of 1NT in exchange for showing distributional hands at the 2-level
teaching.principle: DONT uses conventional overcalls to describe shape (not strength), enabling partner to judge fit and level quickly. The double shows an unknown single suit via a relay mechanism.
teaching.roles: Overcaller describes shape; advancer evaluates fit. After double, advancer must relay 2C to discover the suit.

name: Weak Two Bids (Bundle)
description: Preemptive openings at the 2-level with Ogust 2NT for structured hand description

teaching.purpose: Open light hands with a long suit at the 2-level to preempt opponents, and use the Ogust convention to describe hand strength and suit quality
teaching.whenToUse: Open 2D/2H/2S with 6+ cards in the suit and 5-11 HCP. As responder, use 2NT (Ogust) to ask about opener's hand, raise to game with 16+ HCP and fit, or invite with 14-15 HCP and fit.
whenNotToUse: 2C is reserved for strong openings — never open 2C as a weak two
With a hand too strong for a weak two (12+ HCP) — open at the 1-level instead
After opponent interference — standard competitive actions apply
teaching.tradeoff: 2-level openings lose their natural strong meaning in favor of preemptive action
teaching.principle: Weak twos simultaneously obstruct opponents and describe a narrow hand type (6-card suit, limited strength) that partner can evaluate precisely
teaching.roles: Opener describes; responder captains. After Ogust 2NT, opener further classifies along strength and quality dimensions

name: Michaels / Unusual 2NT (Bundle)
description: Michaels Cue Bids and Unusual 2NT: compete over opponent's suit opening with two-suited hands (5-5+)

teaching.purpose: Show a two-suited hand (5-5 or better) when an opponent opens a suit, enabling partner to choose the best fit at a competitive level
teaching.whenToUse: After opponent opens 1C/1D/1H/1S: cue-bid their suit to show a two-suited hand, or bid 2NT to show both minors. Over a minor opening, the cue bid shows both majors. Over a major, it shows the other major plus an unspecified minor.
whenNotToUse: With only 5-4 shape — need 5-5 or better
With a strong balanced hand — don't confuse with a Western Cue Bid
Against 1NT — use DONT instead
teaching.tradeoff: Michaels sacrifices the natural/strong meaning of the cue bid and 2NT in exchange for immediately showing a two-suited competitive hand
teaching.principle: Showing two suits simultaneously lets partner judge fit quickly. The vulnerability-gated HCP range (looser NV, tighter Vul) reflects the risk/reward of competing.
teaching.roles: Overcaller describes shape; advancer evaluates fit and chooses the best strain and level

name: Strong 2C Opening (Bundle)
description: Strong artificial 2C opening (22+ HCP) with 2D waiting response and structured rebids

teaching.purpose: Open very strong hands (22+ HCP) with an artificial 2C bid that is game-forcing, then describe hand type through rebids
teaching.whenToUse: Open 2C with 22+ HCP. As responder, bid 2D (waiting) with 0-7 HCP, or make a positive response (suit or 2NT) with 8+ HCP. After 2D, opener rebids naturally: suit with 5+, 2NT with 22-24 balanced, 3NT with 25-27 balanced.
whenNotToUse: With 20-21 HCP — open at the 1-level and jump-rebid
With a weak hand and a long suit — use a Weak Two instead
2C is always artificial — never open 2C to show clubs
teaching.tradeoff: 2C loses its natural club meaning in exchange for a forcing opening that prevents being passed out with a monster hand
teaching.principle: The strongest hands need a forcing opening to ensure the auction continues. 2C → 2D (waiting) is the universal relay; opener then describes shape and strength through rebids.
teaching.roles: Opener describes; responder evaluates. After 2D waiting, the auction is game-forcing (except after 2NT rebid with 0-2 HCP).

name: Negative Doubles (Bundle)
description: Negative doubles after partner opens 1-of-a-suit and opponent overcalls — shows unbid suits and competitive values

teaching.purpose: When opponents overcall partner's opening bid, use a conventional double to show the unbid suits (especially majors) and competitive values instead of passing or overbidding
teaching.whenToUse: Partner opens 1C/1D/1H/1S and RHO overcalls. You have 6+ HCP (at 1-level) or 8+ HCP (at 2-level) and length in the unbid suits.
whenNotToUse: You have a natural bid available (5+ card suit you can bid at the appropriate level)
You want to penalize the overcaller — pass and hope partner reopens with a double
The overcall is at the 3-level or higher — negative doubles typically apply through 2S only
teaching.tradeoff: Negative doubles give up the penalty double of the overcall at low levels in exchange for showing the unbid suits efficiently
teaching.principle: After an overcall, double shows the unbid suits (especially unbid majors) with competitive values. The specific suits shown depend on which suits are already bid.
teaching.roles: Responder describes shape via double; opener evaluates fit and rebids accordingly

name: New Minor Forcing (Bundle)
description: After 1m-1M-1NT, bid the other minor as an artificial checkback to find major-suit fits or gauge strength

teaching.purpose: After opener rebids 1NT (showing 12-14 balanced), use the other minor as an artificial forcing bid to find a 3-card major fit or determine the right game contract
teaching.whenToUse: After the auction 1m - 1M - 1NT: bid 2D (if opened 1C) or 2C (if opened 1D) with 10+ HCP to ask opener for more information.
whenNotToUse: With a weak hand (6-9 HCP) — sign off in 2M or pass 1NT instead
When you already know the right contract — bid game directly
After interference — NMF does not apply when opponents have bid
teaching.tradeoff: The other minor loses its natural meaning after 1m-1M-1NT in exchange for a forcing checkback mechanism
teaching.principle: After 1NT rebid, responder may need more information to choose between partscore, invite, and game. NMF provides a one-round force that asks opener to show 3-card major support, the other major, or NT range.
teaching.roles: Responder initiates NMF to gather information; opener describes hand further (major support → other major → NT min/max)
